stem:
3(mx + p) = (24/11)x + 5/7

In the given equation, m and p are constants and p > 0. The equation has no solution. What is the value of m?
rationale: Expanding the left side gives 3mx + 3p = (24/11)x + 5/7. A linear equation has no solution exactly when the coefficients of x are equal while the constant terms differ. So 3m = 24/11, giving m = 8/11. The constant terms then satisfy 3p ≠ 5/7, which is consistent with p > 0 (provided p ≠ 5/21).
